items.prompt: Track A in this course means practice on…
choices: A real local terminal (Linux, macOS, or WSL)
items.explain: Track A is the real Unix / real shell track.

choices: Browser concept labs and quick feedback
items.explain: Track B builds intuition in the browser; it does not replace a real shell for scripts you keep.

items.prompt: You must complete both Track A and Track B for every module.
items.explain: Either track is OK for self-study; both is recommended. Later courses expect real-shell comfort.

items.prompt: A good first rhythm for a lab module is…
choices: Skip the README and only watch video
Browser lab for the idea, then real shell for practice
Only copy commands without a checklist
items.explain: Browser first for intuition, then real Unix for fidelity, is the recommended path.
